title: Oriented laser activated processing chamber
abstract: Implementations described herein provide apparatus and methods for laser-assisted deposition of films while forming electronic devices. In one implementation, a method for depositing a layer on one or more substrates is provided. The method comprises flowing a deposition precursor gas across a surface of the one or more substrates disposed within a processing volume of a processing chamber, thermally activating the deposition precursor gas to deposit a material layer on the surface of the one or more substrates, dissociating an etch precursor gas in a gas activation cell by exposing the etch precursor gas to photons from an energy source assembly having a wavelength selected for pyrolytic dissociation of the etch precursor gas and introducing the dissociated etch precursor gas into the processing volume to etch at least a portion of the material layer from the surface of the one or more substrates.
claims:
Claims Claims: 1 . A processing system, comprising: a processing chamber, comprising: a chamber wall defining a processing volume; a substrate support for supporting one or more substrates within the processing volume; and a gas activation region formed in the processing volume; and an energy source assembly positioned external to the processing chamber and for providing radiant energy to the gas activation region to dissociate the etchant gas, the energy source assembly comprising: an optical cavity having a proximal end and a distal end; a first reflector positioned at the proximal end; a second reflector positioned at the distal end, the optical cavity being defined by the first reflector and the second reflector; and a gain medium disposed axially with respect to the optical cavity for providing optical gain, wherein the energy source assembly produces an axial emission of radiant energy from at least the distal end to the proximal end. 2. The processing system of claim 1 , wherein the gas activation region is located axially with respect to the optical cavity. 3. The processing system of claim 2, wherein the gas activation region is located between the gain medium and the second reflector. 4. The processing system of claim 1 , wherein the gas activation region is defined by a housing and the housing includes a first transparent window and a second transparent window for luminescent coupling of the gas activation region with the axial emission of radiant energy. 5. The processing system of claim 1 , wherein the gain medium is selected from the group consisting of: a doped yttrium aluminum garnet (YAG), sapphire 

 (aluminum oxide), beryl, emerald, ruby, yttrium vanadate, yttrium lithium fluoride (YLF), or glass. 6. The processing system of claim 1 , further comprising a pump source positioned adjacent to the gain medium for providing radiant energy to the gain medium. 7. The processing system of claim 1 , wherein the first reflector and the second reflector are positioned to reflect radiant energy back into the optical cavity and through the gain medium. 8. The processing system of claim 7, wherein the first reflector has between 75% and 98% percent reflection while the second reflector has between 75% and 98% percent reflection. 9. The processing system of claim 1 , wherein the processing chamber includes a first transparent window and a second transparent window opposing the first transparent window for luminescent coupling of the processing volume with the axial emission of radiant energy. 10. A processing system, comprising: a processing chamber, comprising: a substrate support for supporting one or more substrates within the processing chamber; and a gas activation cell coupled to the processing chamber, comprising: a housing defining a gas activation region; an inlet formed in the housing for delivering an etchant gas to the gas activation region; and an outlet formed in the housing for delivering a dissociated etchant gas from the gas activation region to the processing chamber; and 

 an energy source assembly for providing radiant energy to the gas activation region of the gas activation cell to dissociate the etchant gas, the energy source assembly comprising: an optical cavity having a proximal end and a distal end; a first reflector positioned at the proximal end; a second reflector positioned at the distal end, the optical cavity being defined by the first reflector and the second reflector; and a gain medium disposed axially with respect to the optical cavity for providing optical gain, wherein the energy source assembly produces an axial emission of radiant energy from at least the distal end to the proximal end. 1 1 . The processing system of claim 10, wherein the gas activation cell is disposed axially with respect to the optical cavity. 12. The processing system of claim 1 1 , wherein the gas activation cell is positioned between the gain medium and the second reflector. 13. The processing system of claim 10, wherein the housing includes a first transparent window and a second transparent window for luminescent coupling of the gas activation region with the axial emission of radiant energy. 14. The processing system of claim 10, wherein the outlet is coupled to the processing chamber via a conduit having a straight portion extending into the processing chamber. 15. The processing system of claim 14, wherein the conduit is heated.
